We recommended that the Management: a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year, and conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials and prepare summary of supplies and materials at least once a year, and prepare and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; b. Direct the GSO to summarize the Request and Issue Slip (RIS) monthly into a Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit the report monthly to the MAO, and maintain stock cards for inventories and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records with the ledger cards of the MAO; and c. Require the Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory system in the recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials once a year; Prepare summary of supplies and materials monthly; Prepare and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; Summarize Request and Issue Slip (RIS) monthly into Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit to MAO; maintain stock cards; conduct periodic reconciliation; use perpetual inventory system.

Augment personnel to GSO to enable to execute their mandated duties and functions.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory
of supplies and materials at least once a year, and prepare and submit
Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Auditor not
later than January 31 of each year;
b. Direct the GSO to summarize the Request and Issue Slip (RIS) monthly
into a Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit the
report monthly to MAO, and maintain stock cards for inventories and to
conduct periodic reconciliation of their records with the ledger cards of
the MAO; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory system
in the recording of inventories.

2.6. We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all
Local Roads and a Road Map and prepare RLRN and RPCLRN yearly;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting journal entries to
recognize changes upon completion of the required inventory reports and
provide depreciation expense accordingly; and
c. Augment personnel to GSO to enable to execute their mandated duties
and functions.

3.6. We recommended that the Management direct the concerned officials to
pass an Appropriation Ordinance providing funds for the operation and
maintenance of the PRDP subproject and open a separate trust fund account
where the allocated fund be deposited pursuant to Section 7.06 of the
Implementation Management Agreement and Item 2.8 of PRDP Intensified
Building-Up of Infrastructure and Logistics for Development (I-BUILD)
Component Operations Manual.

4.10. We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of
DBM Local Budget Circular 125 in the utilization of the BGCM Fund of
the Municipality/City.

5.8. We recommended that the Management direct the personnel involve to
efficiently identify PPAs and to optimize its implementation to take full
advantage of the benefits derived from these projects.

6.6. We recommended that Management: (1) Post the reports on the receipt and
utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and donations in cash and in-kind
in at least three conspicuous places of the Municipality in the absence of the
LGU-Website; (2) Instruct the GSO to coordinate with the MDRRMO and
other offices with regard the donated items for the maintenance of an
inventory list thereof; (3) Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the required
summary list together with the required supporting documents and submit
the same promptly to COA; (4) Require the MDRRMO/Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit to COA the reports on cash donations
and in-kind donations; and (5) Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2014-
002 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting
guidelines of cash and in-kind donations.

7.7. We recommended that the Management prepare the Annual GAD
Accomplishment Report together with the required supporting documents
cited in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC. No. 2013-01 and submit the same to
COA pursuant to COA Circular 2014-001.

8.5. We recommended that the Management instruct the MPDC and the
Municipal Engineer to coordinate, prepare and submit duly accomplished
list of all on-going government infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs
and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance to COA
Circular No. 2013-004 for the timely and thorough verification of the PPAs’
status.

9.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to comply
on the reporting and posting requirements related to government
procurement pursuant to the GPPB guidelines to ensure transparency and
accountability.

of the said accounts. It is also recommended that the Management issue

Officers in charge to intensify the collection of the said Loans Receivables. It is
also recommended that the Management assign key personnel to monitor the
loan granted to the various recipients.

3.6 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant to request for write-off on the receivables considered dormant and
long outstanding by adhering to guidelines provided by the COA Circular No.
2016-005 should the collection be not possible despite diligent efforts.

4.7 We recommended that the Management:
1. Instruct the accountable officers to return the unserviceable properties to
the property officer;
2. Instruct the property officer to submit request for disposal to the office of
the Auditor concerned with the necessary supporting documents and
commences the public auction of the property to minimize the risk of PPE
being expose to further deterioration;
3. Instruct the Property Officer together with the three (3) AOs of the vehicles
to trace the documents of the grants to fully supply the information in the
RPCPPE;
4. Instruct the property Officer and the MENRO to communicate with the
PENRO-ADS and ask the latter for the necessary documents to support the
turn over of the awarded equipment to the LGU to secure its ownership of
such PPEs, and correspondingly be recorded in the books.

5.5 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer together with the GAD Focal point system
and other officials to spearhead the establishment of GAD Database consisting
sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics to strengthen its implementation
of GAD related activities.
5.6 It is also recommended that the Officer in-charged submit
supporting documents as basis in its GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR)
and optimally utilize the funds set aside for the GAD related activities.
5.7 Lastly, we recommended that the MHO provide justification on the
obligated amount to supplement the repair and maintenance of Ambulance,
which was not in accordance with the GAD AR.

6.7 We recommended that the Management provide budget for the
implementation of Sanitary Landfill and instruct the MENRO to develop more
its implementation of Solid Waste Management in accordance with the
provisions stated in RA 9003 otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste
Management Act of 2000.
6.8 It is also recommended that the MENRO continuously implement
and search for other waste diversion methods that could help minimize or
mitigate the possible hazardous effects of the non-establishment of Sanitary
Landfill.

7.7 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant will require
as part of the supporting documents the pictures of the signage being placed in
the construction site for claims of mobilization fee as the case may be.

8.6 We recommended that the procuring entity submit a copy of the
contract within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular
2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents
peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of
auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
8.7 Likewise, please submit to COA the lacking documents as
enumerated above, to enable the Audit Team to review the contract in its
entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as
true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end
without action.

9.8 We recommended that the Management:
a. Stop the practice of using the 20% Development Fund to open bank
accounts or to use as a maintaining balance since the nature of this
transaction will not partake the nature of either an investment or capital
expenditures as set forth in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated
April 13, 2011; and
b. Return the funds to 20% DF so that it can be used to finance other
important programs and projects of the LGU.

10.7 We recommended that the Management:
a. Include in the Appropriation Ordinance for the year providing funds for
the proper operation and maintenance of the PRDP subproject pursuant
to Section 7.06 of the Implementation Management Agreement; and
b. Monitor and maintain the completed project through implementation of
the activities set forth on its Operation and Maintenance (O & M) Plan to
ensure that the purpose and objectives of the project are continually
served to the constituents.

11.5 We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in
evaluating expenditures that are eligible for funding out of the Confidential
Fund pursuant to Item 3.23 of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 dated January
8, 2015 of the Commission on Audit (COA), Department of Budget and
Management (DBM), Department of the Interior and Local Government
(DILG), Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of National
Defense (DND).

conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Establish the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to observe proper accounting and recording of the said transfer.

9.5   	We recommended that Management:
Stop the practice of transferring funds out of the 20% Development Fund to Trust Fund as LGU counterpart for KALAHI-Makamasang Tugon intended for administrative expenses;
Allocate funds from General Fund Proper for the administrative and operational expenses of KALAHI Office; and
Follow strictly the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided under Section 4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM dated February 22, 2017.

10.7	 We recommended that the management:
Stop charging ineligible expenses to 5% LDRRMF;
Allocate funds for regular operation expenses of the LDRRM Office; and
Follow strictly the guidelines in the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Item No. 4 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2013.

11.5 We recommended that management:
Set aside 1% of Internal Revenue Allotment for the strengthening and implementation of the programs and projects of the LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344.
Select representatives from the youth sector and private agencies set forth in Rule 15.c of the Implementing Rules and Regulation of Republic Act No. 9344; and
Formulate other programs, projects and activities chargeable against LCPC and fully utilize the fund so that children could enjoy its benefits.

12.6 We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll against the GSIS billing statement if monthly amortization for various GSIS loans availed by employees were deducted from their monthly compensation;
Instruct also the Agency Authorized Officer for GSIS to diligently review employees’ loan applications before its approval, and see to it that net take home pay of the member is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization of the loan applied for; and
Strictly deduct monthly GSIS loan amortization against the employees’ compensation pursuant to Section 14 of RIRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid surcharges and penalties to be imposed by the GSIS.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll against the billing statement if monthly amortization for Pag-ibig loans availed by employees were deducted from the monthly salary of the concerned employee; and
Strictly deduct monthly Pag-IBIG loan amortization against the employees’ monthly compensation pursuant to Rule VII of RA No. 9679, to avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that management strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay of not less than Four Thousand pesos (Php4,000.00) to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for to their daily needs until the next payday.

15.6 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to submit a copy of suspension orders for all projects immediately after its issuance to COA Office supported with complete documents to substantiate the reason/s for the suspension and strictly observe the provisions cited in COA Circular No. 80-101-C dated July 28, 1980.

We recommended that management exercise prudence in the disbursement of funds and the practice of letting other associations organize the activities of the municipality as to not circumvent the rules and regulations of the procurement process of the government.

17.5 We recommended that management secure either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract price pursuant to Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

18.8	We recommended that management instruct the implementing office to fast tract the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.
18.9	It is also recommended to include all PPAs implemented and to be implemented in the status report to be submitted to COA for proper monitoring.

19.8  We recommended that Management:
Intensify the utilization of the Gender and Development Budget pursuant to  Section 36 of RA No. 9710 otherwise known as The Magna Carta of Women;
Instruct that GAD Focal Person to reconcile with the Municipal Budget Office as to the incurrence of expenditures for the year in the preparation of its GAD Accomplishment Report; and
Monitor the expenditures made out of GAD appropriations and set controls to establish the reliability of the said disbursements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards maintained in the Treasurer’s Office or the General Services Office.

We recommend that Management require the responsible officials to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009 by informing COA Auditor through written request or submission of delivery receipts of inventories, specifically consumable items on or within 24 hours after delivery of procured inventories or materials for proper checking as to the correctness/accuracy and existence of deliveries.

We recommend that Inventory Committee should conduct actual physical inventory of all types of its PPE annually.

It is also recommended that reconciliation should be made between the actual physical count of the PPE and accounting records, with following procedures:

1. After the physical inventory-taking, the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records.

2. The inventory listing of the equipment shall be checked against the ledger card maintained by the Property and the Ledger cards maintained by the accounting and finally against the control account.

3. All discrepancies between physical and booked PPE must be verified and investigated.

4. Adjustments in the books of accounts will be made by the Accountant, if necessary.
observations.reason:
Physical inventory taking were conducted but huge amount remained unreconciled. There are some accounts of PPEs that needs evaluation and proper classification.

Reiterated in Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management:

1.Require the Supply/Property Officer to maintain Inventory Custodian Slip and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment on the issuance of property and semi-expendable items; and

2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements for the purchase of equipment and semi-expendable items should be supported with ARE or ICS, respectively.
We recommend that the management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries on the affected accounts for proper monitoring of PPE accounts.

We recommend that the management:

1. Strictly adhere to the provisions of NBC No. 442 and NBC 486 relative to the cost of participation to seminars conducted by private institutions to avoid excessive expenditures;

2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the control in reviewing claims and liquidation reports to avoid paying more than what is allowed; and

3. Require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess claims.

We recommend that the management stop the practice of carrying the burden in paying expenses that are incurred due to a request of providing technical assistance to any activities conducted by government organization or associations. However, official time may be granted for this kind of activity.

To forestall the occurrence of similar transactions, we recommend that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of Sec. 93 of PD 1445 and refrain from the practice of paying its obligations thru reimbursement basis to its officers and employees instead of directly paying its suppliers.

We recommend that Management require the Municipality's BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommend that the Management instruct the Municipal Local School Board to:

1. Refrain from utilizing the SEF for ineligible expenses;

2. Stop the practice of paying unbooked obligations / prior years expenditure; and


3. Prioritize the funding of programs, projects and activities stipulated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the Annual and Supplemental Budget for SEF.

We recommend that Management:

1. Stop the practice of transferring funds out of the 20% Development Fund to Trust Fund as LGU counterpart for KALAHI and BUB projects more than the amount required by the National Agency per approved program of work.

2. Stop charging projects and programs not within the Annual Investment Plan.

3. Allocate funds from General Fund for the administrative and operational expenses of KALAHI Office.

4. Fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.
